data.current_description: Creates a hosted payment page session. The customer is redirected to a payabl.-hosted page to complete the payment.
data.description_suggestion: Initiates a hosted payment page session for redirect-based payment flows. Accepts merchant ID, transaction amount, currency, and customer details via PaymentInitRequest. Returns a session object containing a redirect URL that directs the customer to the payabl.-hosted payment page for secure payment completion. The hosted page handles card data collection and PCI compliance. Side effects: Creates a new payment session in pending state; customer must complete payment within the session timeout period.
data.reasoning: Current description lacks critical details for AI agent decision-making. Missing: what inputs are required (merchant ID, amount, currency), what the response contains (redirect URL), and important side effects (session creation, timeout behavior). Enhanced description provides complete context for when and how to use this operation, including the redirect-based flow characteristic of hosted payments.

message: Operation 'getPaymentWidgetSession' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary uses 'Get' which is misleading—the operation creates a session, not retrieves an existing one. Description lacks input details (what PaymentInitRequest contains), specific output details (session ID format, authentication token), and side effects (session state, timeout). Enhanced summary uses action verb 'Create' for clarity. Enhanced description specifies inputs, outputs (session ID and token), and side effects to help AI agents understand the embedded payment flow and session lifecycle.

data.current_summary: Get a Web SDK session ID
data.current_description: Creates a session for the payabl. Web SDK payment widget. Returns a session ID used to initialize the embedded payment form.
data.summary_suggestion: Create Web SDK session for embedded payments
data.description_suggestion: Creates a session for the payabl. Web SDK payment widget to enable embedded payment form integration. Accepts merchant ID, transaction amount, currency, and customer details via WidgetSessionRequest. Returns a session object containing a session ID and authentication token used to initialize the embedded payment widget on the merchant's page. Side effects: Creates a new widget session in pending state; session ID must be used within the specified timeout period to initialize the widget.
data.reasoning: Summary uses 'Get' which is misleading—the operation creates a session, not retrieves an existing one. Description lacks input details (what PaymentInitRequest contains), specific output details (session ID format, authentication token), and side effects (session state, timeout). Enhanced summary uses action verb 'Create' for clarity. Enhanced description specifies inputs, outputs (session ID and token), and side effects to help AI agents understand the embedded payment flow and session lifecycle.

data.current_description: Performs a full authorization and charge on a card. The funds are immediately captured.
data.description_suggestion: Performs a full authorization and immediate capture of funds on a card. Requires merchant ID, card details, amount, and currency. Returns a TransactionResponse containing the transaction ID, status, and authorization code. Funds are immediately debited from the cardholder's account. Requires SHA-1 signature authentication.
data.reasoning: Description lacks details about required inputs (merchant ID, card details, amount, currency), what is returned (TransactionResponse structure), and side effects (immediate fund debit). Added these details for better AI agent understanding of when and how to use this operation.

data.current_description: Reserves funds on a card without capturing. A subsequent capture or partial capture call is required to complete the transaction.
data.description_suggestion: Reserves funds on a card without immediate capture. Requires merchant ID, card details, amount, and currency. Returns a TransactionResponse containing the transaction ID and authorization code. Funds remain reserved but not debited until a subsequent capture or partial capture operation is performed. Requires SHA-1 signature authentication.

data.current_description: Captures the full amount of a previously pre-authorized transaction.
data.description_suggestion: Captures the full reserved amount from a previously pre-authorized transaction. Requires merchant ID, transaction ID from the pre-authorization, and optional amount/currency. Returns a TransactionResponse confirming the capture status. Funds are debited from the cardholder's account upon successful capture. Requires SHA-1 signature authentication.

data.current_description: Captures a partial amount of a previously pre-authorized transaction.

data.current_description: Refunds a previously captured transaction. The amount and currency must be specified.

data.current_description: Cancels or reverses a previously authorized or captured transaction.
data.description_suggestion: Reverses or cancels a previously authorized or captured transaction. Requires merchant ID and transaction ID from the original transaction. Returns a TransactionResponse confirming the reversal status. For pre-authorized transactions, reversal releases reserved funds. For captured transactions, reversal initiates a refund. Requires SHA-1 signature authentication.
data.reasoning: Description is vague about inputs (transaction ID, merchant ID), outputs (TransactionResponse), and side effects (different behavior for pre-authorized vs. captured transactions). Added clarity on the distinction between reversing pre-authorizations and captured transactions to help AI agents understand when to use this operation.

data.current_description: Sends funds to a card (payout). Used for disbursements and refunds to a different card.
data.description_suggestion: Sends funds to a card via credit fund transfer (CFT) for payouts and disbursements. Requires merchant ID, recipient card details, amount, and currency. Returns a TransactionResponse containing the transfer ID and status. Funds are transferred to the specified card account. Commonly used for merchant payouts and refunds to alternative cards. Requires SHA-1 signature authentication.
